hypothesis: If open-weight language models SFTed on BCC/B2 compositions are further tuned with Direct Preference Optimization using Thermo-Calc–derived hierarchical preferences, then they will generate more valid, novel, and thermodynamically favorable BCC/B2 composition pairs that meet the four synthesis objectives at higher rates (with lower lattice mismatch) than SFT-only and prompting/agentic/crystal-generation baselines because physics-based feedback aligns the models’ token distributions toward desired phase behavior while limiting distribution shift (β=0.5).
